foundingOffer.name: 48-Hour Proof Mission
foundingOffer.headline: Give GoalOS one serious AI-delivered work package.

pricing.name: Private Beta
pricing.description: For early proof missions and product feedback.

pricing.name: Proof Mission
pricing.description: One AI-delivered work package reviewed into an Evidence Docket and Mission Receipt.

pricing.name: Team
pricing.description: Shared templates, reviewers, receipt policies, and proof mission operations.

examples.title: AI research report acceptance
examples.input: AI-assisted market, policy, or strategy report.
examples.process: Check claims, source freshness, contradictions, assumptions, and unresolved risks.
examples.output: Evidence Docket, reviewer notes, decision state, signed receipt.
examples.decision: Accept, request changes, reject, or escalate.

examples.title: AI automation delivery review
examples.input: Workflow automation, agent trace, test output, handoff notes.
examples.process: Map acceptance criteria to test evidence, failure cases, and rollback plan.
examples.output: Verifier report, risk ledger, acceptance record, reuse package.

examples.title: Enterprise AI adoption decision
examples.input: Pilot results, stakeholder objectives, constraints, vendor material.
examples.process: Compare baselines, surface risk, map evidence to decision requirements.
examples.output: Executive brief, decision deck, evidence matrix, action graph.
examples.decision: Proceed, revise, defer, or reject with rationale.

examples.title: Market-entry proof mission
examples.input: Market thesis, customer signals, competitor sources, assumptions.
examples.process: Validate demand signals, contradictions, timing, and risk.
examples.output: Claims matrix, source provenance, decision state, action graph.
examples.decision: Commit, test further, pause, or pivot.

examples.title: Grant / milestone verification
examples.input: Milestone deliverable, outputs, links, screenshots, evaluation notes.
examples.process: Map deliverables to acceptance criteria and reviewer obligations.
examples.output: Milestone Evidence Docket and signed Mission Receipt.

examples.title: Procurement or vendor review
examples.input: Proposal, security notes, pricing, references, pilot results.
examples.process: Separate claims from evidence, compare alternatives, surface hidden risk.
examples.output: Vendor proof docket, decision deck, risk ledger, action plan.
examples.decision: Approve, negotiate, reject, or require more proof.

examples.title: Defensive cybersecurity readiness review
examples.input: Repo-owned defensive findings, controls, audit notes, patch proposal.
examples.process: Verify scope, safety boundaries, reproducibility, human review, and remediation path.
examples.output: Defensive Evidence Docket, safety ledger, reviewer-ready plan.
examples.decision: Review, patch, defer, or require external review.

glossary.Mission: The bounded work objective and decision to support.
glossary.Proof Mission: A short, focused engagement that turns one AI-delivered work package into evidence, review, and a decision record.
glossary.Evidence Docket: A public-safe proof room linking claims to evidence, sources, risks, verifier notes, and replay path.
glossary.Verifier report: A structured review of whether claims are supported, incomplete, contradicted, risky, or ready for human decision.
glossary.Mission Receipt: A signed record of the accepted version, evidence hashes, reviewer decision, and public proof boundary.
glossary.Claim boundary: The line between what is supported by evidence and what remains a hypothesis, assumption, or future milestone.
glossary.Governed decision state: An inspectable package containing the objective, claims, evidence, risks, decision, actions, and replay path.
glossary.Chronicle: The durable history of a mission, its proof, decision, and reusable lessons.
glossary.Capability package: What the organization can reuse from an accepted mission: templates, criteria, evidence patterns, and workflow knowledge.
